title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes:
Note that I have been playing the game an hour or so with other versions of WINE before I switched to Proton (for the reasons mentioned below). Proton fixes all of the audio issues that were had in the other versions of WINE, but one takes quite a hit with frame rates. An example is that I had to play on medium settings to get a reasonably stable 60fps, but it still dropped to (at lowest) 6fps when going to new areas. 




ESYNC had to be turned off to get past the title menu.




Tested with other versions of WINE (3.7, 3.12, 3.18 and tkg-3.19 and 4.0 staging) (with DXVK), and needed no turning off esync, however it was plagued with audio issues. Either the voices were too quiet and/or some areas had loud crackling or water rushing. Though these ran perfectly on ultra settings at a steady 60fps (with some massive frame drops when going to new areas, which seems to be somewhat prevalent anyway on the Windows version (based upon reading some forums).
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: Runs perfectly
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: all set to ultra and 1080p runs at 40-50 fps with some fps drops but seams to be a common problem with this game and unreal engine (lots of post processing to simulate senua's psychosis) maybe with "-nod3d11" works better, never tried personally
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: Spent a few hours trying to find the source of crashes early in the game.  Using MATE desktop with Compiz compositing, game would reliably crash after crossing rickety boards high over beach, and would not load save file, requiring staring the game over from the beginning.  Started an i3wm session with no Compiz, and game then started fine at checkpoint save before board crossing.  Continued without issues.
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes:
Full playthrough.

The game used to crash a lot, but it didn't happen once during my few last hours of gameplay. Since the last crash, there has been a Proton update, Nvidia driver updates, and i also manually upgraded DXVK to 0.96. One of those three things must have fixed the issue.

Audio works out-of-the-box with the latest Proton version. I heard a teeny tiny bit of crackling in a very specific area (the bridge to Hela's gate, because it's a windy area I guess), but besides that it's flawless.

For some odd reason, running in fullscreen mode makes the KDE taskbar overlap the game. Hiding the taskbar or running in fullscreen window fixes the issue.

No issues with controller support, both my F310 and my PS4 controller work and rumble is supported.
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: The game is running very well, I'm playing for the first time and it seems all right, the presets are all in the very high, some stutering in loading parts, but nothing to get in the way. I recorded a bit of how I'm running it. https://youtu.be/sgdTPpJSsjw
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes:
Had to install FAudio 19.02 (https://github.com/Kron4ek/FAudio-Builds/releases/tag/19.02) in the Wine prefix to fix the audio lag in cinematic sequences, now the game runs perfectly. No crackling or other audio issues.
Performance is surprisingly good. Slight stutter when loading new areas for the first time (probably until shaders are cached), otherwise mostly stable 30 FPS using the lowest setings with 1080p on a system below the minimum requirements.
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: Tested a few different performance levels and I've played the game up until the halfway point. Works very well, with the only minor bug being a small desync in the cinematic audio.

title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: The launch options "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" were the only way I could get the game to first launch on my system at all, and then start a new game without crashing. While the game loads and is technically playable in the short time I have spent playing it through Proton, there is a lot of graphical glitching/artifacting that really impedes my enjoyment of the game.  Hopefully I'll be able to find a fix for the graphics issues and will report back if I do.
title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes: It worked for me at first startup. Only issue I had were minor stuttering at cut scenes but overall I enjoyed the game play and had no major problems. I was limited to medium settings by my budget system but still got a playable 50-60fps. Good enough for me anyway. Disabling VSync helped some more.  Seem to be having a lot more luck lately with latest proton version.

notes: Game works perfectly out of the box with 32GB(4x8GB) RAM. With 16GB(2x8GB) the game crashes during intro logo splash screens. I suspect the problem has something to do with running dual-channel memory on Threadripper, as with quad-channel the game works fine. 16GB should have been more than enough to launch the game. I'm playing it safe and rating this Gold for now, it is possible that this is not a Proton issue at all but rather something else like Kernel/Linux/CPU/Motherboard/BIOS etc..

notes: Had to run "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" to be able to start a new game without crashing to desktop. The main problem is persistent crashing at the bridge near the start, which occasionally produces an error with D3D "LowLevelFatalError" failing to load shaders. Tested without launch commands but can't start a new game without. Tested with different proton versions, and all fullscreen/windowed options and graphical presets but can't progress further (I'm below spec but so are others who have had more success).

title: Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice

notes:
Completed the entire game without graphical glitches. Aside from the rare and minor issue of audio getting out of sync in cutscenes due to shader compilation stutter, there were no issues with the game.

The game is very demanding, and FPS usually hovered around 50-60 FPS with high settings with 1440p resolution. This seems to be similar to what you'd get on Windows, though.
